blurb_en:
"Hidden Track" — The Atlantic exposed the training data secret the music industry has been trying to find for three years. Investigative journalist Alex Reisner published four searchable databases totaling over 21 million songs scraped for AI training without licensing or consent: 12 million from LAION, 9 million from a dataset assembled by Sleeping AI researchers. Taylor Swift, Bad Bunny, and virtually every major artist are in there.

• The Atlantic made the databases searchable so any artist can now check if their work was included.
• LAION's 12 million-song dataset was assembled without licensing agreements of any kind.
• Sony Music is still litigating against Suno and Udio; UMG settled with Udio in October 2025.
• The training data includes songs from artists whose record labels have active suits against the exact companies that trained on them.
• For Insta360: music licenses every travel and product video — the next copyright fight may determine whether creators can legally soundtrack their own work.

Three years of lawsuits, and the industry finally has a map of what was taken.
blurb_zh:
《大西洋》月刊曝光了音乐行业寻找了三年的训练数据底细。调查记者 Alex Reisner 发布了四个可检索数据库，收录超过 2100 万首歌曲，均未取得授权：其中 LAION 贡献 1200 万首，Sleeping AI 研究团队贡献 900 万首。泰勒·斯威夫特、Bad Bunny 以及几乎所有主流艺人都赫然在列。

• 《大西洋》将数据库设置为可检索，艺人可自查自己的作品是否被纳入。
• LAION 的 1200 万首曲库在未签署任何授权协议的情况下组建完成。
• 索尼音乐仍在起诉 Suno 和 Udio；环球音乐已于 2025 年 10 月与 Udio 和解。

三年诉讼，这张账单终于被翻了出来。
verification_notes: The Atlantic original report by journalist Alex Reisner confirmed via Music Ally (June 16) and Engadget (June 16). 21M+ song figure confirmed across both outlets. LAION 12M and Sleeping AI 9M figures confirmed. Taylor Swift and Bad Bunny inclusion confirmed. Sony litigation and UMG-Udio settlement confirmed via prior reporting.

blurb_en:
"Attention Shift" — The man who co-invented the mechanism that made modern AI possible has moved to the company that built the most visible product from it. Noam Shazeer, co-author of the 2017 "Attention Is All You Need" paper, has joined OpenAI as Lead for AI Architecture Research. Google paid roughly $2.7 billion to bring him back from Character.AI in 2024. He stayed just over a year.

• Sam Altman said on X he had wanted to work with Shazeer "since the early days."
• Shazeer announced the move himself on X on June 17.
• The transformer architecture he co-invented underpins GPT, Gemini, Claude, Llama — essentially every large language model in production today.
• Google's $2.7 billion re-acquisition now looks like a holding pattern that couldn't hold.

The talent arms race has reached the people who wrote the foundational paper.
blurb_zh:
发明了让现代 AI 成为可能的核心机制的人，刚刚加入了将这一机制做成最有影响力产品的公司。《注意力就是一切》（Attention Is All You Need，2017）联合作者 Noam Shazeer，以 AI 架构研究负责人的身份加入 OpenAI。谷歌于 2024 年花了约27亿美元从 Character.AI 把他挖回来；这一次他待了刚刚一年多。

• Sam Altman 在 X 上表示，他“从早期就想和 Shazeer 合作”。
• Shazeer 于 6 月 17 日在 X 上自行宣布了这一消息。
• 他联名发表的这篇论文，奠定了 GPT、Gemini、Claude、Llama 等几乎所有主流大模型的架构基础。
• 谷歌 27 亿美元的回购，现在看来不过是一次拖延。

人才争夺战已经打到了写基础论文的人。
verification_notes: Shazeer move confirmed via TechTimes (June 18) and multiple outlets. Sam Altman X post confirmed. Shazeer X announcement June 17 confirmed. $2.7B Character.AI acquisition by Google confirmed via prior reporting (2024). Role as Lead for AI Architecture Research confirmed.

blurb_en:
"Case Study" — The first federal appeals court to hear an AI training copyright case did so June 11, quietly, with almost no press coverage. Thomson Reuters vs. Ross Intelligence asks whether Westlaw's legal headnotes are copyrightable and whether using them internally to train an AI search tool constitutes transformative fair use. The Third Circuit's answers will become binding precedent and a powerful signal for every AI copyright case that follows.

• Ross built a legal search competitor by training on Westlaw headnotes — summaries lawyers wrote to explain case law, not raw court text.
• Two core questions on appeal: are those headnotes copyrightable? Does internal AI training count as transformative use?
• The District Court already ruled against Ross in 2023; this is the appeal.
• Unlike image and music cases, this involves text humans created specifically to explain law — not raw creative expression.
• The Ninth Circuit, which covers most California-based AI companies, will likely follow or diverge from whatever the Third decides here.

The most important AI copyright case most people haven't heard of just had its day in court.
blurb_zh:
联邦上诉法院首次就 AI 训练版权争议进行口头辩论，时间是 6 月 11 日，几乎没有媒体报道。汤森路透诉 Ross Intelligence 案的核心问题：Westlaw 的法律摘要（headnotes）是否受版权保护？将其用于训练 AI 法律搜索工具是否构成合理使用？第三巡回法院的裁定将成为辖区内有约束力的先例，并对全国所有 AI 版权案产生深远影响。

• Ross 用律师撰写的 Westlaw 法律摘要训练了一个竞争性法律搜索产品，而非直接使用原始案例文本。
• 地区法院已于 2023 年判决 Ross 败诉，此次为上诉审。
• 不同于图像和音乐案件，本案涉及的是人工专门为解释法律而创作的文字。

这是最重要却最少人知道的 AI 版权案，刚刚完成了庭审辩论。
verification_notes: Oral argument date June 11 confirmed via legal news aggregator and World Trademark Review. Thomson Reuters v. Ross Intelligence Third Circuit case confirmed. District Court 2023 ruling against Ross confirmed. Core fair use questions confirmed from legal coverage. Confidence medium because original Third Circuit docket not directly accessed.

blurb_en:
"Good Fetch" — Anthropic put Claude Opus 4.7 in control of a Boston Dynamics Spot robot dog and told it to complete a real-world robotics challenge. It outperformed the best human-aided team by roughly 20x. The researchers were not robotics specialists: they plugged in a laptop and typed a prompt.

• Opus 4.7 identified the optimal sensor interface quickly and produced working code on the first try for most tasks.
• The human comparison team needed specialist robotics engineers; the Opus team needed a charging cable.
• Phase 2 result: still could not fetch a beach ball, due to limitations handling complex real-time visual feedback loops.
• Anthropic named the project "Fetch." The failure is funnier because of it.
• For Insta360: AI-controlled camera platforms that navigate space autonomously are exactly where robotic rigs are heading — the gap between "can identify a sensor interface" and "can operate a gimbal in a crowd" is closing.

The AI beat the engineers at the engineering, then got stumped by a beach ball.
blurb_zh:
Anthropic 让 Claude Opus 4.7 接管一台波士顿动力 Spot 机器狗，完成一项实际机器人任务。结果：比最强人类辅助团队快了约 20 倍。实验参与者不是机器人专家——他们只是插上笔记本，输入了一个提示词。

• Opus 4.7 迅速锁定最优传感器接口，大多数代码一次跑通。
• 对比的人类团队需要机器人专家；Claude 团队只需要一根充电线。
• 第二阶段结果：仍然无法取回一个沙滩球——实时视觉反馈太复杂，模型还没过关。
• Anthropic 把这个项目命名为“Fetch”（取物/叫狗取物）。失败本身因此显得格外有喜感。
• Insta360 相关：AI 控制相机台架自主导航空间，正是机器人摄影设备的未来方向——“能识别传感器接口”和“能在人群中操控稳定器”之间的差距，正在快速缩小。

AI 在工程任务上赢了工程师，然后被沙滩球绊住了。
verification_notes: Anthropic research blog post confirmed. 20x performance vs human team confirmed. Beach ball failure confirmed. Non-robotics researchers confirmed. Claude Opus 4.7 model confirmed.
